channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sorry American 3D printing fans - Bambu Lab has a mighty new seven-nozzle printer, but you won't be able to get it anytime soon
item.description: Bambu Labs H2C introduces a magnetic seven-nozzle system with improved purge handling, reduced bed size, higher costs -but there's no US release yet.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: France vs Australia free streams: How to watch Autumn International 2025, TV Channels, Preview & Team News
item.description: All the ways to watch France vs Australia live streams online, as Les Bleus host the Wallabies at Stade de France for their 2025 Autumn International closer.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: Expert Edition: How to assemble the building blocks of impact-driven AI
item.description: AI experts at AWS, Dell, Red Hat and ThunderCat share how the right infrastructure, data governance and workforce readiness can successfully shape mission-driven innovation.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: The National Armaments Consortium is gearing up for 2026 with fresh direction and a focus on armaments innovation
item.description: "There are a lot of foreign customers now that are all looking to buy U.S. Weapons systems, so that'll be good for the industrial base," said Ben Harris.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft has yet to ignite enthusiasm for agentic AI
item.description: It’s the year of the agentic enterprise, according to Microsoft, which at its Ignite show this week positioned autonomous workflows and reasoning-driven automation as the almost-inevitable path that enterprises must eventually take.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Discount your AI ROI because mileage always varies
item.description: Everyone has been car shopping and noticed the miles per gallon printed on the window sticker. You get excited when you see a number like 35 MPG, but once you drive the car home, your dashboard seems to stay closer to 26.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fake ChatGPT apps are hijacking your phone without you knowing
item.description: Fake AI apps disguised as "ChatGPT" and "DALL·E" are flooding app stores with dangerous malware that steals data and monitors users without detection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Verizon is laying off over 13,000 workers
item.description: US mobile network giant Verizon will end 13,000 worker contracts, along with many more outsourced workers.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: F# 10 features scoped warning suppression
item.description: The newest version of Microsoft’s multi-paradigm language features a much-sought ability to suppress warnings in specified code sections.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Craigslist car report scam targets vehicle sellers
item.description: Fake buyers demand specific vehicle reports from unknown sites to steal credit card information from car sellers, but warning signs can help identify these scams before paying.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CIO Essential Insights: The New Security Priorities
item.description: Join host John Gallant for a deep dive into key threats and operational priorities that security pros are focusing on for the coming year.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What are TPUs? Your guide to tensor processing units and AI acceleration
item.description:
The gist TPUs are Google’s specialized ASICs built exclusively for accelerating tensor-heavy matrix multiplication used in deep learning models.
TPUs use vast parallelism and matrix multiply units (MXUs) to achieve significantly higher throughput and energy efficiency than GPUs for training.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cisco initiative targets device security
item.description: Cisco is announcing a security initiative that will push customers to update or replace aging infrastructure components, such as routers, switches and firewalls, as well as discourage them from using any insecure features.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A drying-up Rio Grande basin threatens water security on both sides of the border
item.description: Researchers have completed a full accounting of water uses and losses in the Rio Grande-Bravo basin as it faces severe shortages throughout its range in the United States and Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How synthetic data trains AI to solve real problems
item.description: You’ve just finished a strenuous hike to the top of a mountain. You’re exhausted but elated. The view of the city below is gorgeous, and you want to capture the moment on camera. But it’s already quite dark, and you’re not sure you’ll get a good shot.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: AI CEOs are promising all-powerful superintelligence. Government insiders have thoughts
item.description: Tech giants are making grand promises for the AI age. The technology, we are told, might discover a new generation of medical interventions, and possibly answer some of the most difficult questions facing physics and mathematics.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Crypto’s path to legitimacy depends on the industry itself, not just politicians
item.description: President Trump recently promised to make America the “crypto capital of the world.” And his administration is working hard to make that pledge a reality. White House officials have established a working group on digital asset markets and directed federal agencies to craft a strategy to cement U.S.
item.dc:creator: Drew Johnson, Fast Company

item.title: How pairing SAST with AI dramatically reduces false positives in code security
item.description: The promise of static application security testing (SAST) has always been the “shift-left” dream, catching vulnerabilities before they ever hit production. But for too long, that promise has been undermined by a frustrating reality with an overwhelming volume of alerts and high false-positive rates.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Roblox bid for ad riches collides with wary sponsors, developers
item.description: Roblox Corp. executives have been trying to get advertisers to market their wares in the gaming platform’s virtual worlds since the company went public in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Cisco, IBM team to build large-scale quantum networks
item.description: Cisco and IBM plan to pool their R&D efforts to build a network of large-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computers capable of working together to run computations up to hundreds of thousands of qubits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft now lets customers run agents on Windows 365 cloud PCs
item.description: Microsoft has unveiled a new type of Windows 365 cloud PC that provides a secure environment for running “computer use” AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sony, Warner and Universal sign AI music licensing deals with startup Klay
item.description: The world's biggest music labels have struck AI licensing deals with a startup named Klay Vision, the companies said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How to alienate cloud customers and undermine revenue
item.description: Big Tech has entered an arms race to dominate the artificial intelligence space, with Amazon, Google, and Microsoft investing more than $600 billion into AI development between 2023 and 2025.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Azure HorizonDB: Microsoft goes big with PostgreSQL
item.description: Enterprises need data, and data needs to be stored, with a flexible, portable environment that scales from developers’ laptops to global clouds. That storage also needs to be able to run on any OS and any cloud without breaking the bank. There aren’t many options.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft rolls out Agent 365 ‘control plane’ for AI agents
item.description: Microsoft Agent 365 has been introduced as a control plane to help organizations deploy and manage AI agents at scale. Unveiled November 18, Agent 365 is available through Microsoft’s Frontier program for early access to AI technologies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Gemini 3 may be the moment Google pulls away in the AI arms race
item.description: Google announced its widely anticipated Gemini 3 model Tuesday. By many key metrics, it appears to be more capable than the other big generative AI models on the market.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company
